Title: Stacked mutations in multi-copy genes enhance AHAS sulfonylurea herbicide resistance in soybean

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Essential amino acid biosynthesis in plants; target pathway of AHAS-inhibiting herbicides
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Acetohydroxyacid synthase catalytic subunit; thiamine diphosphate-dependent enzyme
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: P182S and P182Y edited alleles confer herbicide resistance with distinct spectra: P182S gives stronger resistance to pyrazosulfuron-ethyl, whereas P182Y gives stronger resistance to bispyribac-sodium; edited alleles retain imazethapyr resistance background
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 cytosine base editing; Sanger sequencing; whole-plant herbicide resistance assays; RT-qPCR; recombinant protein enzyme assay with pyruvate consumption; in silico docking
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Detected in leaves at V1 stage; transcript abundance not markedly altered in mutants versus WT
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Leaves at first trifoliolate stage (V1) analyzed by qPCR
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Parsley ubiquitin promoter (constitutive) used to express nCas9-PmCDA1 editor
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Without herbicide treatment, no major penalty in leaf area or dry weight versus WT; some mutant lines were slightly taller
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Greenhouse growth; pyrazosulfuron-ethyl treatment at 100 g ha−1; bispyribac-sodium at 36 g ha−1; imazethapyr at 70 g ha−1
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Targeted deep sequencing of edited loci; no public accession reported
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for engineering soybean with resistance to multiple AHAS-inhibiting herbicides via allele-specific editing and mutation stacking
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Base editing of GmAHAS2 generated P182S and P182Y alleles. 2. P182S conferred stronger resistance to pyrazosulfuron-ethyl than P182Y. 3. P182Y conferred stronger resistance to bispyribac-sodium than P182S. 4. Recombinant GmAHAS2 P182S retained higher pyruvate-consuming activity than WT in the presence of pyrazosulfuron-ethyl.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Primary experimental focus is herbicide resistance, not direct nutrient enhancement, although AHAS is a key enzyme in branched-chain amino acid biosynthesis.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Essential amino acid biosynthesis in plants; target pathway of AHAS-inhibiting herbicides
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Acetohydroxyacid synthase catalytic subunit; thiamine diphosphate-dependent enzyme
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: P172S contributes to pyrazosulfuron-ethyl resistance; combined P172S/R174C with GmAHAS2 P182Y improves dual resistance to pyrazosulfuron-ethyl and bispyribac-sodium
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Off-target base editing identification; Sanger sequencing; whole-plant herbicide resistance assays; RT-qPCR; in silico docking
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Detected in leaves at V1 stage; transcript abundance not markedly altered in mutants versus WT
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Leaves at first trifoliolate stage (V1) analyzed by qPCR
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Parsley ubiquitin promoter (constitutive) used to express nCas9-PmCDA1 editor
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No clear growth penalty under untreated conditions
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Greenhouse growth; pyrazosulfuron-ethyl, bispyribac-sodium, imazethapyr treatments as above
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Targeted deep sequencing of edited loci; no public accession reported
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be stacked with GmAHAS2 mutant alleles to broaden herbicide resistance spectrum
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Off-target editing generated GmAHAS3 P172S and P172S/R174C alleles. 2. The 3S line was resistant to pyrazosulfuron-ethyl. 3. The 2Y3SC line carrying GmAHAS3 mutations plus GmAHAS2 P182Y showed enhanced resistance to both pyrazosulfuron-ethyl and bispyribac-sodium.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Contribution of R174C versus P172S in the 3SC allele could not be separated experimentally.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Essential amino acid biosynthesis in plants; target pathway of AHAS-inhibiting herbicides
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Acetohydroxyacid synthase catalytic subunit; thiamine diphosphate-dependent enzyme
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: P180F in stacked 2S3S4F line is associated with strongest pyrazosulfuron-ethyl resistance among tested serine-containing mutants, suggesting additive or stronger contribution to resistance
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Off-target base editing identification; Sanger sequencing; whole-plant herbicide resistance assays; RT-qPCR; in silico docking
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Detected in leaves at V1 stage; transcript abundance not markedly altered in mutants versus WT
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Leaves at first trifoliolate stage (V1) analyzed by qPCR
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Parsley ubiquitin promoter (constitutive) used to express nCas9-PmCDA1 editor
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious untreated growth penalty reported
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Targeted deep sequencing of edited loci; no public accession reported
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potentially valuable as a stacked allele with GmAHAS2 and GmAHAS3 for stronger sulfonylurea resistance
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Off-target editing generated GmAHAS4 P180F. 2. The triple mutant 2S3S4F showed the largest leaf area under pyrazosulfuron-ethyl treatment, indicating strongest resistance among tested PE-resistant lines. 3. Docking predicted markedly weakened pyrazosulfuron-ethyl binding to P180F mutant.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Effect of GmAHAS4 P180F was only tested in a stacked background, not as a single mutant.
